comment.body:
Before you trade, make sure you’ve read all the necessary information and truly understand it.
Using that information, formulate a plan—factor in your risk capital, your VaR, and define what you’re betting on and which signals could invalidate that bet.
Then, stick to your plan.

comment.body:
In my post yesterday, I wrote that Trump is under significant pressure from his own partners, which China is exploiting against him.
That pressure caused yesterday’s sudden spike and led the Fed to buy Treasuries. Trump’s decision was impulsive—even he’s startled by the international consequences of a deflationary spiral. I stressed that in yesterday’s post.

comment.body:
Meanwhile, he knows he cannot fold his cards too early, or all will be in vain. 
And I don’t think he actually did fold. The bond-buying was on the Fed’s side, and the tariff delay (10% etc.) didn’t really ease anything. We’re effectively still in the same place—maybe even worse—because tariffs aren’t that diversified now, and they’re heavily weighted on China.

comment.body:
Anyway, as I keep saying: the market is not out of the woods yet. I hope none of you seriously believed an intraday 10% pump on an auction was legit.
It’s a very strong bet on MoM 0% CPI, core CPI, giving the Fed room to cut. Keep it in mind, though personally, I don’t think that’s correct. Deflation fears are still needed. Nothing’s changed—in fact, things are worse.
I’ll spell it out in the post.
Big boys still aren’t buying.
